pick.rationale: Iowa State enters as a legitimate top-7 team with an elite defense ranking 6th nationally in defensive rating (95.6) and an SRS of 5th, while Kentucky sits 28th-32nd in major metrics—a gap of roughly 25 spots that translates to approximately 6 points per 100 possessions. The Cyclones' suffocating defense should neutralize a Kentucky offense that ranks just 39th-40th nationally and is significantly shorthanded without starting point guard Jaland Lowe (season-ending shoulder surgery) and guard Kam Williams (broken foot). Iowa State proved its elite status by taking #2 Arizona to the wire in a heartbreaking buzzer-beater loss in the Big 12 semifinals and dominated Tennessee State 91-42 in the first round, while Kentucky needed a late push to survive Santa Clara and was swept by Florida this season. The combination of Iowa State's defensive prowess, Kentucky's injury-depleted backcourt, and the substantial analytical gap makes this a straightforward 2/7 matchup with minimal upset risk.
